artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-01-05
artifacts.excerpt:
What I think may not be obvious about vasocomputation is that this is a muscle, and some of it is semi-permanently clenched (“latched”), and you would feel ~10x better if it wasn’t

But there is not a science of wholesome and careful latch-opening yet https://t.co/I7twI37jnV

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2024-02-16

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2024-12-02
artifacts.excerpt:
Last year I proposed “vasocomputation,” that vascular tension acts as a special type of memory that regulates neural dynamic range. Recently at @joinedgecity I shared some updates: how ‘thoughts’ are patterns of vascular tension, and implications for Buddhist enlightenment

1/x https://t.co/FOSQWaxoaj

artifacts.title: System receipt from 2025-01-02
artifacts.excerpt:
Niacin is notorious for its flush reaction; opening blood vessels

Niacin (nicotinic acid) injections stop LSD trips within minutes

Pretty suggestive evidence for vasocomputation, and that psychedeics’ characteristic effects arise from their impact on the vascular system… https://t.co/L0ULT3DWnY

artifacts.title: System receipt from 2024-12-27
artifacts.excerpt:
My origin story for humanity: the particular cognitive capacities of modern humans came from being able to flex (and latch) our vasomuscular system in more areas, with greater precision & integration, and with greater strength & duration than other animals

The vasomuscular… https://t.co/fNsPhE4BDY

planets.name: Consciousness
planets.summary: Archive cluster around consciousness: 122 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Consciousness receipt from 2024-03-01
artifacts.excerpt:
New essay about the framerate of consciousness: if life is a sequence of moments, how big are the moments?

Neuroscience has approached the frame-rate of minds via differentiability — if a picture is flashed for 50ms does it affect cognitive processing? What about 10ms?

(1/6)
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: consciousness, research, here, qualia.

artifacts.title: Consciousness receipt from 2025-03-25
artifacts.excerpt:
Julian Jaynes’ “Breakdown of the Bicameral Mind” can be framed as the boundary of human consciousness having shifted within recorded history

Each hemisphere is highly specialized, but my reading of Jaynes’ model is that they were also separately *conscious*. Two semi-sovereign… https://t.co/13hKtTkr4e
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: consciousness, research, here, qualia.

artifacts.title: Consciousness receipt from 2024-11-04
artifacts.excerpt:
Building a realtime AR overlay of the EM field for the Apple Vision Pro or Quest would resolve so many questions about consciousness, and pose new ones

I suspect there’s a substantial “hardware overhang” — we have good enough sensors, we just need to do the integration + UX https://t.co/JNyzd14c09
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: consciousness, research, here, qualia.

artifacts.title: Consciousness receipt from 2023-06-28
artifacts.excerpt: Machine consciousness should be among the most important technical problems of our time, but “consciousness discourse” is subtly anchored in ingroup/outgroup distinctions. If something is ingroup it can probably feel; if it’s outgroup it can’t.

artifacts.title: Latches receipt from 2024-12-30
artifacts.excerpt:
Considering a fresh hypothesis about dreams: “dreaming” is a search for sensory permutations that will trigger release conditions for obsolete latches

There’s no scientific consensus on why we dream, although I really like @erikphoel’s idea that they generate synthetic data so… https://t.co/TUSUO5B1CY

artifacts.title: Latches receipt from 2025-01-25
artifacts.excerpt:
Probably a lot of path-dependency in how our nervous systems get configured

Human experience is a bunch of layered “Tetris effects”, top-down priors on structure

These start as vascular clenches; some clenches get latched, and some of those latches never open https://t.co/ABghFI0oWY
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: latches, latch, vascular, muscle.

artifacts.title: Latches receipt from 2024-12-02
artifacts.excerpt:
@drmichaellevin @JoinEdgeCity We likely latch a specific cluster of networks when we’re ~1-3yo as we interact with and differentiate from The Other, and this semi-permanent tension constitutes the “self”. Most likely these latches are somewhere deep in the brain or brainstem

https://t.co/GGuW1GsXj6 h/t Romeo
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: latches, latch, vascular, muscle.

artifacts.title: Latches receipt from 2024-10-05
artifacts.excerpt:
The vasocomputation take is we encode priors in vascular tension: restrict bloodflow to an area and nearby neurons can’t update. Psychedelics can stochastically “pop latches” and remove the prior — this can take things in good, bad, and/or weird directions
https://t.co/vzGxzCVukw https://t.co/ff18p9rP1b

artifacts.title: Could receipt from 2024-05-03

Legend has it that a famous philosopher literally invented silent reading. I.e. everybody was reading out loud; he figured out you could do it without making sound.

(1) somatic patterns can be viral
(2) what similar decoupling hasn't been invented yet but could be? https://t.co/Qm5OBJ1n5f https://t.co/SjDw3yFiKP
